# Coco

> Coco is a fast, native macOS launcher. One global hotkey opens a single
> panel for launching apps, clipboard history, quick math, unit and currency
> conversion, emoji, web search, shell command aliases, and plugins.

## Facts
- Platform: macOS 14 (Sonoma) or later, Apple Silicon
- Size: ~2 MB; written in Swift + AppKit (no Electron)
- Price: free
- Updates: silent automatic updates via Sparkle, signed appcast feed
- Languages: English, Simplified Chinese, Japanese, Korean (follows system)
- Default hotkeys: ⌥Space (launcher), ⇧⌘V (clipboard); rebindable
- Author: YAN (https://x.com/yan_ai_labs)

## Capabilities
- App launcher with fuzzy search incl. pinyin / romaji / Hangul-initial
- Clipboard history and favorites with preview and paste-back
- Inline calculator
- Unit conversion and live currency conversion
- Emoji search and web search
- User-defined shell command aliases
- JavaScript plugins via the in-app Coco Store

## Install
1. Download https://coco.butterflydream.ai/Coco-macos.dmg
2. Drag Coco into Applications
3. First launch: right-click Coco -> Open (ad-hoc signed; Gatekeeper asks once)
4. Press ⌥Space
